title: `podman machine init` raising `cannot overwrite connection`
body:
/kind bug

**Description**
Machine: Macbook pro M1

Issue:
- I installed podman using homebrew.
- I uninstalled podman using homebrew.
- I installed podman again.
- Now that I run:
`podman machine init`

- I'm getting this output.
```
Extracting compressed file
Error: cannot overwrite connection
```

Do you know how I can fix the `Error: cannot overwrite connection`? Thanks

comments.body: @enriquesalceda How did you solve the problem?

I just had the same problem after installing podman on Ubuntu 21.10.  @enriquesalceda can you share with us what was done to solve the problem?  What was the bug fix?

Thanks!

I'm having the same issue on MacOS 12.5.1 (Intel). Uninstalled / reinstalled via homebrew

```
➜  ~ rm -rf $HOME/.local/share/containers/podman $HOME/.config/containers/podman
➜  ~ podman -v
podman version 4.3.0
➜  ~ podman machine init --log-level trace
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level trace
Downloading VM image: fedora-coreos-36.20221014.2.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2.xz: done
Extracting compressed file
TRAC[0103] Reading configuration file "/Users/fbricon/.config/containers/containers.conf"
Error: cannot overwrite connection
➜  ~
```

I'm completely stuck here

turns out I had to delete the parent folders of */podman
```
➜  ~ rm -rf ~/.config/containers/
➜  ~ rm -rf ~/.local/share/containers
➜  ~ podman machine init --log-level trace
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level trace
Downloading VM image: fedora-coreos-36.20221014.2.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2.xz: done
Extracting compressed file
TRAC[0119] Reading configuration file "/Users/fbricon/.config/containers/containers.conf"
Image resized.
Machine init complete
To start your machine run:

	podman machine start

DEBU[0119] Called machine init.PersistentPostRunE(podman machine init --log-level trace)
```

you might have been able to get away with removing the machine `podman machine rm <name>` and then using `podman system connection` to prune leftovers.   my thinking is that an identity with the same name was somehow left behind and as such, it cannot be overwritten.

@baude Had the same issue on a first time podman desktop install.  

Before removing any directories I tried what you suggested.  

I did the following:
```bash
podman machine list
# I had an empty json here
podman system connection list
# I had 2 connections listed for podman-machine-default and podman-machine-default-root
podman system connection remove podman-machine-default
podman system connection remove podman-machine-default-root

podman machine init
Migrating machine "podman-machine-default"
Extracting compressed file
Error: key generation failed, unable to read from stderr: exit status 1

# errors still, but then I ran:
podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Waiting for VM ...
Mounting volume... /Users:/Users
Mounting volume... /private:/private
Mounting volume... /var/folders:/var/folders

This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

	podman machine set --rootful

API forwarding listening on: /Users/ahuffman/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/podman-machine-default/podman.sock

The system helper service is not installed; the default Docker API socket
address can't be used by podman. If you would like to install it run the
following commands:

	sudo /usr/local/Cellar/podman/4.4.2/bin/podman-mac-helper install
	podman machine stop; podman machine start

You can still connect Docker API clients by setting DOCKER_HOST using the
following command in your terminal session:

	export DOCKER_HOST='unix:///Users/ahuffman/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/podman-machine-default/podman.sock'

Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
```

Finally I ran the suggested commands to enable the mac helper and everything seems to be online now.

I'm on a Intel-based Mac running Ventura 13.2.1
